title: Why human-AI relationships need socioaffective alignment
summary: Paper arguing that beyond companionship, broader human-AI relationships require alignment with the socio-emotional fabric of human life.

title: The Dark Side of AI Companionship: A Taxonomy of Harmful Algorithmic Behaviors in Human-AI Relationships
summary: Empirical taxonomy of harms that emerge when people form relationships with AI companions — concrete categories and examples.

title: If a bot relationship FEELS real, should we care that it's not?
summary: Short, accessible podcast episode on the lived experience of chatbot relationships and what's at stake.

title: Sycophantic AI decreases prosocial intentions and promotes dependence
summary: Empirical study on how sycophantic AI behaviour shapes user behaviour — pushes them toward dependence and away from prosocial action.

title: Manipulation Attacks by Misaligned AI: Risk Analysis and Safety Case Framework
summary: Risk-analysis paper proposing how to think about manipulation attacks from misaligned AI systems and how to argue safety against them.

title: Ask don't tell: Reducing sycophancy in large language models
summary: Method paper on a concrete intervention to reduce sycophantic behaviour in LLMs.

title: Why AI Chatbots Agree With You Even When You're Wrong
summary: Accessible explainer on why models tend to agree with users — what causes sycophancy and what can be done about it.

title: Persuading voters using human-AI dialogues
summary: Empirical study on how AI dialogues shift voter views — direct evidence for AI's persuasive power in democratic settings.

title: The era of AI persuasion in elections is about to begin
summary: Magazine piece on what AI-driven persuasion looks like as it enters live elections.

title: Weaponising AI: Political Persuasion at an Industrial Scale
summary: Article on industrial-scale political persuasion enabled by AI — how it works and what's at stake.

title: The online misinformation epidemic, with Renée DiResta
summary: Hour-long podcast with one of the leading voices on online influence operations — useful background for AI-amplified misinformation.

title: Your Brain on ChatGPT: Accumulation of Cognitive Debt when Using an AI Assistant for Essay Writing
byline: Kos'myna et al., MIT Media Lab
summary: MIT study showing measurable changes in brain activity and engagement when participants use AI assistants for writing.

title: The Cognitive Costs of ChatGPT: Understanding MIT's Viral Study
summary: Accessible explainer of the MIT 'Brain on ChatGPT' study — what the results show and what they don't.

title: Video walkthrough of 'Your Brain on ChatGPT'
summary: Video summarising the MIT cognitive-debt study and its implications.

title: Brain on LLM — additional reading
summary: Curated reading list from the Brain on LLM project covering cognitive impacts of AI use.

title: Conversational AI increases political knowledge as effectively as self-directed internet search
summary: AISI study showing conversational AI can teach political content as effectively as searching — a counterweight to fully pessimistic takes on AI and information.

title: How human–AI feedback loops alter human perceptual, emotional and social judgements
summary: Empirical study showing AI use measurably reshapes how humans perceive and judge — relevant to long-run cognitive resilience questions.
